text: Nitrogen-containing heteroaromatics are of great importance to the field of chemical biology and drug discovery. While advances have been made to achieve the selective modification through both elongation and shortening of the heteroaromatic ring, few methods have been reported that achieve direct atom-for-atom substitution of nitrogen atoms in these compounds. We now report the efficient isotropic exchange (14)N to (15)N of a wide range of aromatic nitrogen heterocycles via the "nitrogen transmutation" protocol, which relies on N-triflylation, a C–N ring-opening and ring-closing sequence catalyzed by (15)N-aspartate with subsequent formation of an isolable (15)N-succinyl derivative and the consequent (15)N-labeled heterocycle through a neutral elimination process. All reactions were performed at mild conditions (room temperature) with excellent isolated yields and high (15)N-enrichment levels of the final products.
explanation: The text reads like a polished chemistry abstract but contains unusual phrasing (e.g., 'isotropic exchange' instead of 'isotopic exchange') and a possibly fictional protocol name, which together suggest a moderate-to-high likelihood of AI authorship.
